headline: IBM (IBM) Reaches First Quantum Milestone In Fusion Material Design
summary: IBM (NYSE:IBM), Oak Ridge National Laboratory and Cleveland Clinic completed the first quantum computer driven computations for fusion energy material design. The work positions IBM at the center of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission for quantum centric supercomputing. The collaboration targets molecular configurations critical for fusion reactors, an area that classical computing has not addressed. For investors following IBM, this move places the company’s quantum computing...

content.summary: IBM (NYSE:IBM), Oak Ridge National Laboratory and Cleveland Clinic completed the first quantum computer driven computations for fusion energy material design. The work positions IBM at the center of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission for quantum centric supercomputing. The collaboration targets molecular configurations critical for fusion reactors, an area that classical computing has not addressed. headline: Oak Ridge National Lab, Cleveland Clinic, and IBM Achieve First-Known Computations of Fusion Materials on a Quantum Computer
summary: A team of scientists from Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), Cleveland Clinic, and IBM (NYSE: IBM), have calculated nine molecular configurations of a promising material to produce fuel for fusion energy – the first-known instance of such computations on quantum computers.

content.title: Oak Ridge National Lab, Cleveland Clinic, and IBM Achieve First-Known Computations of Fusion Materials on a Quantum Computer
content.summary: A team of scientists from Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), Cleveland Clinic, and IBM (NYSE: IBM), have calculated nine molecular configurations of a promising material to produce fuel for fusion energy – the first-known instance of such computations on quantum computers.

thumbnail.caption: Neutrons from fusion plasma strike a molten salt blanket to produce tritium — this material is now being modeled with quantum computers. (Credit: IBM)
